zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor於1527年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可福雷辛費爾德時，她的父親Richard Grenling28歲，母親Rose Barber24歲。她於大約1543年與Sir Nicholas Barber II在英國素福爾可福雷辛費爾德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1571年4月30日在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉可費爾德享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor于1527年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可福雷辛費爾德时，她的父亲Richard Grenling28岁，母亲Rose Barber24岁。她于大约1543年与Sir Nicholas Barber II在英国素福爾可福雷辛費爾德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1571年4月30日在英国英格兰素福爾可拉可費爾德享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Girling
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of   Gerling  .
Probably an altered form of German Gierling, itself a variant of Gerling .

variants.name: Gerling
meanings: German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name beginning with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’; compare Gerl .

English (Suffolk): from the Middle English personal name Gerling, Girling. It is related to ancient Germanic Gerlo, Girlo, and is likewise a pet form of ancient Germanic names in gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’ (compare Gerard and Gerald ).

nameMeanings.name: Barber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a barber, from Middle English barb(o)ur ‘barber’ (Anglo-Norman French barber, Old French barbier, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). In the Middle Ages barbers not only cut hair and shaved beards, but also practised surgery and pulled teeth.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name from German Barbier ‘barber’ (compare 1 above).
Catalan: occupational name for a barber, barber (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Berber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Salvador, Guillermo, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Luis, Adriana, Alfredo, Angelina, Armando, Carlos.
Catalan: probably a variant of   Barber  . This surname is most common in Mexico.
Turkish: occupational name from berber ‘barber’.

variants.name: Barbero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ricardo, Alvino, Ana, Angel, Arsenio, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Maribel, Mario, Nenita.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Domenic, Ernani, Giulio, Italo.  French Emile, Yves.

Spanish and Italian (Piedmont): occupational name for a barber-surgeon, barbero, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’ (from Latin barba); see also Barber . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Barbieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Aldo, Cosmo, Dino, Domenic, Geno, Giovanna, Guido.
Italian: occupational name from barbiere ‘barber-surgeon’ (see Barber ), from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbour
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: occupational name from Old French barbeor ‘barber’. Compare the English form Barber .

History: George Barbour sailed from England on the Transport in 1635. He became a freeman of Dedham, MA, in 1647 and settled eventually at Medfield. A Scottish merchant named Barbour settled in eastern VA in the latter half of the 17th century.

variants.name: Borbely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Antal, Ferenc, Ildiko, Kalman, Laszlo, Miklos.
Hungarian (Borbély): occupational name for a barber, Hungarian borbély. See also Barber .

variants.name: Barbeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Monique, Alcide, Alphonse, Amiee, Armand, Gisele, Jean Francois, Lucien, Lucienne, Michel.
French:

from barbeau ‘barbel’, a type of fish, hence a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, or a nickname for a man with a sparse beard, the fish being distinguished by beardlike growths on either side of its mouth.

nameMeanings.name: Barbor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:
from the Old French female personal name Barbot, a diminutive of Barbe , a short form of Barbara .
nickname for someone who tended to speak indistinctly, from French barboter ‘to babble’, or for someone with a beard, from a diminutive of Old French barbe ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).
French (Barbé): adjectival derivative of 1, meaning ‘bearded’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor was born in 1527, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Grenling, was 28 and her mother, Rose Barber, was 24. She married Sir Nicholas Barber II about 1543, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 30 April 1571, in Laxfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor est née en 1527, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Grenling, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Rose Barber, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Nicholas Barber II environ 1543, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 avril 1571, à Laxfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor 1527 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Grenling 28 und ihre Mutter Rose Barber 24. Sie hat um 1543 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England Sir Nicholas Barber II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. April 1571 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Laxfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor è nata il nel 1527 nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard Grenling, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Rose Barber, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir Nicholas Barber II circa 1543, nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 30 aprile 1571, nel Laxfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barborが1527年にイギリスイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドで生まれた時，父Richard Grenlingは28歳，母Rose Barberは24歳であった。約1543年にイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドでSir Nicholas Barber IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1571年4月30日にイギリスイギリススフォルクラクスフィールドで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor(이)가 1527년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프레징필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Grenling(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rose Barber(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1543년 영국 수폴크 프레징필드에서 Sir Nicholas Barber II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1571년 4월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 라스필드에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor nasceu em 1527, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Grenling, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Rose Barber, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Nicholas Barber II aproximadamente 1543, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de abril de 1571, em Laxfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor родилась в 1527 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Grenling было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Rose Barber, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Nicholas Barber II около 1543 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 30 апреля 1571 г. в местности лаксфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor nació en 1527, en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Grenling, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Rose Barber, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Sir Nicholas Barber II aproximadamente 1543, en Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 30 de abril de 1571, en Laxfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 44 años.
